docs.title: Hi, I'm Himani
docs.text:
I'm a technical writer with 2+ years of experience creating end-to-end documentation for complex software and developer platforms. I specialize in DITA-XML, structured authoring, API documentation, GitBook, and content systems.

Currently at Xplor Technologies, I lead documentation for the Xplor Pay Voyager Developer Center — covering merchant onboarding, payment workflows, and compliance requirements. I'm passionate about translating complex technical workflows into developer-friendly content.

docs.title: Professional Bio
docs.text:
I'm a technical writer based in New Delhi, India, specializing in end-to-end documentation for complex software and developer platforms. With 2+ years of experience, I've worked with global companies like Xplor Technologies and Synopsys to create documentation that bridges the gap between engineering and end users.

My background in Computer Science (MSc Informatics) gives me the technical depth to understand complex systems, while my expertise in structured authoring and API documentation ensures that clarity flows outward to users of all skill levels.

After working with DITA-XML at Synopsys for over two years, I've seen firsthand how structured authoring transforms documentation workflows. Here's why it matters and how to get started.

docs.title: Automating Documentation Workflows with Python
docs.text:
April 15, 2026 :tags: automation, Python, docs-as-code

One of the most impactful things I did at Synopsys wasn't writing documentation — it was automating the processes around it. I developed Python and Bash scripts to automate OLH (Online Help) build check-ins, reducing manual effort by 40%.

Here's how I approached it.

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
Every time we updated documentation, the OLH build process required several manual steps:

  1. Build the help files from DITA-XML source
  2. Run validation checks
  3. Check the output into Perforce
  4. Verify the build succeeded
  5. Notify the team

This process took 30+ minutes per cycle and was prone to human error.

docs.title: The Solution
docs.text:
I wrote a Python script that:

  1. Triggers the Build: Calls the DITA-OT (DITA Open Toolkit) with the correct parameters.
  2. Validates Output: Checks that all expected files were generated without errors.
  3. Automates Check-in: Uses Perforce command-line tools to commit the build output.
  4. Notifies the Team: Sends a confirmation message with build status.

docs.title: Example Script Structure
docs.text:
import subprocess
import sys

def build_olh(project_path):
    """Build OLH from DITA-XML source."""
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["dita", "-i", f"{project_path}/main.ditamap", "-f", "htmlhelp"],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"Build failed: {result.stderr}")
        sys.exit(1)
    print("Build successful")

def check_in_to_perforce(output_path, changelist):
    """Check OLH output into Perforce."""
    subprocess.run(["p4", "add", f"{output_path}/..."])
    subprocess.run(["p4", "submit", "-d", f"OLH build: {changelist}"])
    print("Checked in successfully")

build_olh("/path/to/project")
check_in_to_perforce("/path/to/output", "Automated OLH build")

docs.title: The Results
docs.text:
• 40% reduction in manual effort
  • Zero build errors from human mistakes
  • Faster turnaround from content update to published help
  • More time for actual documentation work

docs.title: Key Takeaways
docs.text:
1. Identify Repetitive Tasks: If you do it more than three times, automate it.
  2. Start Simple: Begin with one workflow and expand from there.
  3. Use What You Know: I knew Python, so I used Python. Start with the tools you're comfortable with.
  4. Measure Impact: Track time saved to justify further automation efforts.

docs.title: The Bottom Line
docs.text:
Technical writers who can automate are exponentially more valuable. You don't need to be a software engineer — you just need to think like one when it comes to your own workflows.

Want to discuss documentation automation? Find me on LinkedIn or GitHub.

docs.title: Structured Authoring with DITA-XML: Why It Matters
docs.text:
May 1, 2026 :tags: DITA-XML, structured authoring

When I started working at Synopsys as a Technical Product Publication Engineer, I quickly learned that good documentation isn't just about what you write — it's about how you structure it.

Here's why DITA-XML and structured authoring matter, and why every technical writer should understand them.

docs.title: What Is DITA-XML?
docs.text: DITA (Darwin Information Typing Architecture) is an XML-based architecture for authoring, producing, and publishing technical information. Unlike unstructured formats like Word, DITA enforces a content model that ensures consistency, reusability, and multi-channel output.

docs.title: 1. Content Reuse
docs.text: Write once, publish everywhere. DITA's topic-based architecture means you can reuse content across multiple outputs — user guides, online help, release notes — without duplication.

docs.title: 2. Consistency
docs.text: DITA's structured elements enforce consistent documentation patterns. Every concept, task, and reference follows the same structure, making it easier for readers to find what they need.

docs.title: 3. Multi-Channel Output
docs.text: Generate PDFs, HTML, online help, and mobile content from a single source. DITA's transformation pipelines handle the formatting, so you focus on content.

docs.title: 4. Scalability
docs.text: As your documentation grows, DITA scales. Large documentation sets that would be impossible to maintain in Word become manageable with structured content and conditional profiling.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Learn the Basics: Understand topics, maps, and DITA elements.
  2. Use Oxygen XML: The industry-standard editor for DITA authoring.
  3. Start Small: Convert one document type to DITA and learn the workflow.
  4. Build Templates: Create reusable topic templates for your team.

docs.title: The Bottom Line
docs.text:
Structured authoring isn't just a technical skill — it's a mindset shift from document-centric to content-centric thinking. Once you make that shift, everything about documentation becomes more efficient.

Have questions about DITA-XML or structured authoring? Reach out on LinkedIn or GitHub.

docs.title: APIwiz Technical Writing Assessment
docs.text: Client: APIwiz (Interview Process) Role: Technical Writer Candidate Type: Documentation Skills Assessment

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Completed a practical technical writing assessment for APIwiz as part of the interview process. The assessment evaluated skills across three core documentation disciplines: user guide creation, FAQ writing, and troubleshooting guide development.

docs.title: Task 1: Docuwiz User Guide
docs.text: Brief: Create a comprehensive user guide manual for Docuwiz.io, following the Microsoft Writing Style Guide with clear, concise, and user-focused language.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Account creation and onboarding flow
  • Platform modules: Guides, References, Recipes, Categories, Workspace Settings
  • Creating, managing, and publishing content
  • User journey format — step-by-step navigation for new users

docs.title: Writing Standards Applied
docs.text:
• Active voice and present tense throughout
  • Consistent tone, terminology, and formatting
  • Proper headings, lists, notes, and callouts
  • Industry-standard documentation practices

docs.title: Tools Used
docs.text: Google Docs, Microsoft Writing Style Guide

docs.title: Task 2: TaskFlow FAQ Writing
docs.text: Brief: Write a comprehensive FAQ section (minimum 15 entries) for the TaskFlow project management tool, addressing real user complaints and questions from the support team.

docs.title: Sample Questions Covered
docs.text: # Question Answer 1 Why can't I log in? Ensure you're using the correct email and password. If you've forgotten your password, use the "Forgot Password" link on the login page to reset it. If the issue persists, check that your account hasn't been locked due to multiple failed attempts. 2 How do I assign tasks to others? Open the task and click the "Assignees" field. Start typing the team member's name and select them from the dropdown. You can assign multiple members to a single task. 3 How do I change my password? Go to Workspace Settings > Account > Change Password. Enter your current password, then your new password, and confirm the change. 4 What is the difference between Free and Paid plans? The Free plan includes up to 5 projects, 10 members per project, and basic task management features. Paid plans unlock unlimited projects, advanced reporting, integrations, priority support, and more. 5 How do I delete my account? Go to Workspace Settings > Account > Delete Account. Note that this action is permanent and will remove all your data, projects, and task history. 6 Why am I not receiving email notifications? Check your notification preferences in Settings > Notifications. Ensure the correct email address is verified. Also check your spam folder — some email providers may filter notification emails. 7 How many people can be added to one project? The Free plan allows up to 10 members per project. Paid plans support up to 100 members per project, with enterprise plans offering unlimited members. 8 Does TaskFlow work on mobile? Yes, TaskFlow is fully responsive and works on any mobile browser. Native iOS and Android apps are also available for download. 9 How do I export tasks to PDF? Open the project or task list, click the "Export" button, and select PDF format. You can choose to export individual tasks or the entire project view. 10 How do I set task priority? Open the task, locate the "Priority" dropdown, and select Low, Medium, High, or Critical. You can also set priority from the task list view by clicking the priority icon. 11 How do I create a subtask? Open the parent task and click "Add Subtask" under the Subtasks section. Subtasks inherit the parent's project, assignees, and due date by default. 12 How do I archive completed tasks? Mark a task as complete, then click the "Archive" button in the task menu. Archived tasks are hidden from the main view but can be accessed in the Archive section. 13 How do I share a project with external members? Go to Project Settings > Members > Invite External. Enter the guest's email address and select their permission level (Viewer or Commenter). External members receive an invitation email. 14 How do I change my profile picture? Click your avatar in the top-right corner, select "Profile Settings," and upload a new image. Supported formats are PNG, JPG, and GIF (max 2 MB). 15 How do I use filters in the dashboard? Click the filter icon in the dashboard toolbar. You can filter by status, priority, assignee, due date, and custom tags. Multiple filters can be combined for precise results. 16 What happens to my data if I cancel my subscription? Your data remains accessible in read-only mode for 30 days after cancellation. During this period, you can export your data or reactivate your subscription. After 30 days, the account and data are permanently deleted. 17 How do I restore deleted tasks? Go to the Trash/Recycle Bin from the project menu. Select the task you want to restore and click "Restore." Tasks are permanently deleted after 30 days in the trash. 18 Does TaskFlow integrate with Slack or Google Calendar? Yes, TaskFlow offers native integrations with Slack, Google Calendar, Microsoft Teams, and Zapier. You can enable integrations from Workspace Settings > Integrations. 19 How do I set task reminders? Open the task and toggle "Set Reminder" under the Due Date section. Choose when you want to be notified — options include 1 hour, 1 day, or 1 week before the due date. 20 What is the difference between Admin and Member roles? Admins have full access to project and workspace settings, including billing, member management, and deletion permissions. Members can create and manage tasks but cannot modify workspace-level settings.

docs.title: Task 3: TaskFlow Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: Brief: Write a complete troubleshooting guide for TaskFlow, covering 18 documented issues with a quick reference table, note/tip/warning callouts, and a contact support section.

docs.title: Quick Reference Table
docs.text: # Issue Symptom Solution 1 App crashes on large file uploads App freezes or closes when uploading files over 10 MB Compress files before uploading. Use the desktop app for large transfers. 2 Login fails with special characters Authentication error when password contains special characters Reset password using alphanumeric characters only. Password rules have been updated in version 2.5. 3 Dashboard not loading on Internet Explorer Blank page or layout errors on IE Switch to a supported browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari). Internet Explorer is no longer supported. 4 Email notifications in spam Notification emails consistently land in spam folder Add noreply@taskflow.com to your address book. Whitelist the domain in your email settings. 5 Tasks not syncing across devices Changes made on mobile don't appear on desktop Pull-to-refresh on mobile. Ensure both devices are on the latest app version. Syncing occurs every 30 seconds. 6 Dark mode not working on older Android UI remains in light mode on Android 8 and below Dark mode requires Android 9 or later. Update your device OS if possible, or use the light theme. 7 PDF export fails for long descriptions Export button does nothing when task has extensive description Split the task into smaller subtasks and export individually. Descriptions over 10,000 characters may cause export failures. 8 Search fails with combined filters Empty results when multiple filters are applied simultaneously Remove filters one at a time to identify conflicts. Some filter combinations are incompatible in the current version. 9 Attachment limit reached "Maximum 5 attachments" error Remove unused attachments before adding new ones. Consider linking to cloud storage for additional files. 10 Video files not supported Error when attaching video files Convert videos to a link format (YouTube, Vimeo, cloud storage share link). Video files are not currently supported as direct attachments. 11 Slow performance on large projects App lag when project exceeds 500 tasks Archive completed tasks to reduce active task count. Use filters to limit the visible task set. Upgrade to a paid plan for priority performance. 12 Two-factor authentication code missing SMS or authenticator app does not show the 2FA code Ensure your phone has network connectivity. Check your authenticator app's time sync. Contact support if the issue persists. 13 Auto-logout after inactivity Session expires after 10 minutes of inactivity This is a security feature for shared devices. To extend your session, check "Keep me signed in" during login. Session timeout can be adjusted in workspace settings (admin only). 14 Calendar view empty for tasks without due dates Tasks with no due date do not appear in calendar view Calendar view only displays tasks with assigned due dates. Set a due date for each task to make it visible on the calendar. 15 Google Calendar sync fails in certain time zones Sync errors for users in UTC+5:30 and other non-standard offsets Verify that your device time zone matches your TaskFlow profile time zone. Manual re-sync may be required after daylight saving time changes. 16 Subtasks missing from main task view Subtasks are created but cannot be seen in the main list Enable "Show Subtasks" from the View Options menu. Subtasks are hidden by default in the main list view to reduce clutter. 17 Bulk export taking too long Export progress bar stalls for large projects with 1,000+ tasks Large exports are processed in batches. For faster results, export by filtered subsets (by status, assignee, or date range). 18 Notifications not clearing on mobile Read notifications remain in the notification center on mobile app Swipe left on the notification to dismiss it. Pull-to-refresh to sync the read status. The web version clears notifications automatically.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
Prevent Common Issues

Keep your app updated to the latest version. Regularly archive completed tasks to maintain performance. Use supported browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) for the best experience.

Data Safety

Always export important data before making significant account changes. Deleted tasks are retained in the trash for 30 days — this is your window for recovery.

Version Compatibility

TaskFlow version 2.5+ includes fixes for many of the issues listed above. Check your app version in Settings > About and update if available.

docs.title: Contact Support
docs.text:
If you experience an issue not covered in this guide, or if the suggested solutions do not resolve your problem, please contact TaskFlow Support:

  • Email: support@taskflow.com
  • In-App: Help > Contact Support (includes diagnostic information)
  • Response Time: Within 24 hours on business days

When contacting support, please include:

  1. Your TaskFlow account email
  2. Device and browser information
  3. Steps to reproduce the issue
  4. Any error messages or screenshots
  5. Your TaskFlow version number (Settings > About)

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
This assessment demonstrates proficiency in three core technical writing skills:

Skill Deliverable User Guide Creation Docuwiz onboarding and feature documentation following Microsoft Writing Style Guide FAQ Writing 20 FAQ entries addressing real user pain points with clear, concise answers Troubleshooting 18 documented issues with quick reference table, callouts, and escalation path

docs.title: AI Text-to-Diagram Generator
docs.text: Project Type: Personal/Professional Development Year: 2025 Role: Developer & Technical Writer

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text: Documentation teams often struggle to create visual diagrams quickly. Traditional diagramming tools require manual effort and design skills. There was a need for a tool that could convert natural language descriptions into professional, editable diagrams.

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
1. Full-Stack Development: Built the platform using React.js, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS, and D3.js for the frontend.
  2. Mermaid Integration: Leveraged Mermaid.js for diagram rendering with support for multiple diagram types.
  3. SVG Export: Implemented export functionality for documentation team workflows.
  4. User-Centered Design: Designed an intuitive interface for technical and non-technical users alike.

docs.title: Technologies
docs.text: React.js, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS, D3.js, Mermaid, SVG

docs.title: Impact
docs.text:
• Streamlined diagram creation for documentation teams
  • Reduced time-to-diagram from minutes to seconds
  • Export support for integration into existing documentation workflows

docs.title: OLH Build Automation at Synopsys
docs.text: Client: Synopsys Inc. Duration: Sep 2023 – Dec 2025 Role: Technical Product Publication Engineer

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text: The Online Help (OLH) build check-in process was entirely manual, consuming significant time and introducing opportunities for human error. The team needed an automated solution to streamline this critical workflow.

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
1. Script Development: Developed Python and Bash scripts to automate the OLH build check-in process.
  2. Process Mapping: Analyzed the existing manual workflow to identify automation opportunities.
  3. Testing & Validation: Ensured the automated process produced identical results to the manual workflow.

docs.title: Impact
docs.text:
• 40% reduction in manual effort for OLH build check-ins
  • Eliminated human error in the build process
  • Freed up time for higher-value documentation work

docs.title: Tools Used
docs.text: Python, Bash, Git, Perforce

docs.title: End-User Documentation & Release Notes at Synopsys
docs.text: Client: Synopsys Inc. Duration: Sep 2023 – Dec 2025 Role: Technical Product Publication Engineer

docs.title: Challenge
docs.text: Synopsys required comprehensive end-user documentation for complex EDA (Electronic Design Automation) tools. The documentation needed to cover user guides, installation guides, application notes, and Quick Tip videos — all created using structured authoring with DITA-XML.

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
1. Structured Authoring: Created end-user documentation and release notes using DITA-XML in Oxygen XML, ensuring consistency across all content types.
  2. UI/UX Writing: Applied UI/UX writing principles to craft intuitive user-centered content for web and in-app experiences.
  3. Full Documentation Lifecycle: Managed the complete process from requirement gathering through drafting, review, and publishing.
  4. Cross-Team Collaboration: Worked with R&D, AE, and QA teams for technical insights and product update alignment.
  5. Multimedia Content: Produced Quick Tip videos using Camtasia, SnagIt, and iSpeak for visual learners.

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• User guides with DITA-XML structured content
  • Installation and configuration guides
  • Application notes for specific use cases
  • Quick Tip video tutorials
  • Release notes for each product version
  • Online Help systems

docs.title: Impact
docs.text:
• Reduced manual OLH build check-in effort by 40% through automation
  • Improved documentation analytics (CTR, engagement, top pages) to guide content strategy
  • Consistent content across all documentation types through structured authoring

docs.title: Tools Used
docs.text: DITA-XML, Oxygen XML, Camtasia, SnagIt, iSpeak, Jira, Confluence, CMS tools
